events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights
events.description: The United Nations is raising alarm over continued drone attacks in Sudan’s Darfur region after multiple strikes reportedly killed civilians and intensified fears for communities already trapped by the brutal conflict between rival militaries.

events.title: Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns
events.description: Nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence were recorded worldwide last year – more than double the previous year's figure – as rape, sexual slavery and abduction were deployed as weapons of war across Africa, the Middle East, Europe and the Caribbean.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns

events.title: World News in Brief: Lebanon violence continues, Israel ‘severs ties’ with UN chief, Internal displacement in Haiti, warning to France over prison overcrowding

events.title: Lula says Brazil will not be treated like ‘tinpot country’ after US designates gangs as terrorists
events.description: Marco Rubio made announcement after meeting president’s far-right challenger Flávio Bolsonaro Brazil will not be treated as a “tinpot country,” the country’s president, Luiz Inácio da Silva, said on Friday after the United States designated Brazil’s two largest criminal gangs, the First Capital Command (PCC) and the Red Command, as foreign terrorist organisations. The announcement, made by Marco Rubio, the secretary of state, on Thursday, is being widely seen in Brazil as a setback for Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, the president who had strongly opposed the designation – and a boost for Lula’s main challenger in October’s presidential election, the far-right senator Flávio Bolsonaro. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lula says Brazil will not be treated like ‘tinpot country’ after US designates gangs as terrorists

events.title: Heavy rains threaten to delay search for 2 people missing in Laos cave

events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heavy rains threaten to delay search for 2 people missing in Laos cave

events.title: WHO puts Ebola outbreak death rate at ‘huge’ 30-50% as chief arrives in DRC
events.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus calls for ceasefire among armed groups to help avoid deaths from preventable disease The death rate of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is between 30% and 50%, the World Health Organization has said, as its head arrived in the country to support efforts to contain the disease. Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases. “It’s huge. It means that up to five out of 10 people are likely to die,” Legand told reporters in Geneva. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO puts Ebola outbreak death rate at ‘huge’ 30-50% as chief arrives in DRC

events.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study
events.description: ‘Megafires’ in California, Canada, South Korea and Europe in 2025, but changes to farming slowed spread in parts of Africa “Devastating” wildfires ripped across the wealthier parts of the world in 2025, a study has found, even as globally, the area ravaged by flames fell. Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of African farms that have fragmented landscapes and hampered the spread of large savannah fires. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study

events.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
events.description: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief

events.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
events.description: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find them Four more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them. The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
events.description: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia launched a massive barrage of missiles and drones against multiple Ukrainian cities. Moscow has since threatened further sustained strikes. Sharp divisions among Council members are expected, with European nations demanding an immediate ceasefire while Russia insists its strikes target only military infrastructure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting

events.title: Ethiopia heads to the polls: Elections under the shadow of war and dissent
events.description: As Ethiopia prepares to hold its seventh General Elections on June 1, Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed and the ruling Prosperity Party are widely expected to secure a landslide victory. But the stakes for him are high nonetheless: restoring stability and projecting an image of national unity. Critics warn that growing restrictions on press freedom and political dissent are casting a shadow over the vote. And with 70% of the country still affected by armed conflict, many question whether the conditions for a truly democratic election are in place. Report on the ground by our correspondent Tom Canetti.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Ethiopia heads to the polls: Elections under the shadow of war and dissent

Emergency services were called to Newbury Street in Werribee just after midnight on Monday. Continue reading...

The warning came as the US, UK and Australia announced a new Aukus project to develop new underwater drone technology to protect undersea cables. Continue reading...

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
events.description: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by a broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters

events.title: Colombia prepares to go to polls in election shadowed by resurgence of political violence
events.description: Sunday’s presidential vote is contest between left and right – and between contradictory proposals for dealing with the decades-long armed conflict Mateo Pérez Rueda was one internship away from completing a degree in political science. The 24-year-old also worked as a bicycle delivery rider and sold fruit salads and juice to finance his passion: the Colombian independent digital magazine El Confidente . On 4 May he travelled to Briceño, in the western province of Antioquia, to report on the long-running conflict between the army, paramilitaries and dissidents of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Continue reading...

events.title: Australia news live: Hastie says falling house prices might not be ‘all that encouraging’ for recent buyers; 13-year-old allegedly planned attack on Queensland school
events.description: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tony Abbott, the new party president of the Liberals, said he wouldn’t get “too excited” by the poll showing One Nation’s surging support. But he told RN this morning he believes the country is in trouble and the Liberal/National Coalition is best positioned to lead the nation. Abbott spoke to RN Breakfast this morning, saying: Our economy is stagnant, our society is fragmenting, our security is imperiled, and yes, we don’t believe in ourselves nearly enough. I think the current government is making all of this worse. And it’s really important that we get a better government as soon as possible. … Obviously, we are in a degree of competition with other parties and voices on the centre-right. But in the end, our opponent, our enemy, if you like, is a really bad Labor government, a really bad Green-left-Labor government. Which in its budget, [has led an] assault on aspiration and wealth creation. The point I make is: if you normally vote Liberal, if you’re interested in public life, if you think our country is in trouble, please don’t complain on the sidelines. Join the party and make a difference. Continue reading...

events.description: Ukrainian drones struck targets across several Russian regions overnight from Saturday, including an oil pumping station, a refinery and a fuel depot, Russian and Ukrainian authorities said Sunday. Kyiv however denied Moscow's claims that a Ukrainian drone struck the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant in Russian-occupied Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Ukraine hits Russian energy targets, denies striking Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant

events.title: WHO chief hails 5 Ebola recoveries as a new treatment center opens in eastern Congo
events.description: The World Health Organization reports that five patients have recovered from a rare type of Ebola in eastern Congo

events.title: Man wanted in deputy ambush caught after wildlife camera sighting and major manhunt
events.description: Michael Puckett, accused of killing Virginia Deputy Logan Utt in an ambush during a welfare check, was captured in North Carolina after a multistate manhunt and $60,000 reward.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Man wanted in deputy ambush caught after wildlife camera sighting and major manhunt

events.title: US says it struck Iranian radar sites as Kuwait reports missile and drone attacks
events.description: Iran and the US say they have carried out fresh attacks around the Strait of Hormuz, the third known escalation in a week.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US says it struck Iranian radar sites as Kuwait reports missile and drone attacks

events.title: Banning children from social media is not enough, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design
events.description: Blocking children from social media is no substitute for making platforms safe in the first place, the UN human rights office warned Friday, as it issued a 10-point framework urging governments and tech companies to go further and faster to protect children online.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Banning children from social media is not enough, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Death of Congolese man renews scrutiny of race relations in Ireland
events.description: Yves Sakila died after being restrained by security guards ‘in broad daylight’ Irish authorities have agreed to a second postmortem on the body of a Congolese man who died after being restrained by shop security guards on a Dublin street, prompting an outcry and comparisons to the death of George Floyd. A forensic pathologist from England is to conduct an independent postmortem this week on Yves Sakila, 35, an alleged shoplifter who was pursued and pinned to the ground in the city centre on 15 May. The police force, An Garda Síochána, is investigating. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Death of Congolese man renews scrutiny of race relations in Ireland

events.title: Brazil is set to cut in on China's rare earths dominance
events.description: Brazil is seeing increasing demand for its vast reserves of rare earth elements. Global mining giants are betting on Brazil as the next powerhouse for critical magnetic metals and an alternative to China's dominance.

events.title: Five recover from Ebola - as Brazil investigates two suspected cases
events.description: Five people have recovered from a rare type of Ebola virus, the head of the World Health Organisation said - as suspected cases were investigated in Brazil and Italy.

events.title: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow
events.description: Every day in Kharkiv begins with uncertainty: air raid sirens interrupt sleep; missiles strike residential neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and roads. Anxious citizens rush into metro stations during bombardments and children study underground.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow

events.title: UN honours blue helmets and calls for renewed investment in peace
events.description: Peacekeepers are serving in a dangerous era of mounting global tensions and tight resources. UN chief António Guterres is now calling for greater political backing and reliable financial support for ‘blue helmets’ who put themselves in harm’s way.

events.title: UN80 Initiative: ‘Critical new phase’ for UN reform effort
events.description: UN chief António Guterres has outlined further progress in the wide-ranging UN80 Initiative, a major reform and restructuring project aimed at making the global body more effective, agile and better equipped to respond to global challenges.

events.title: Dead whale towed ashore in Denmark ahead of autopsy
events.description: "Timmy" was found dead off the coast of Anholt despite multiple attempts to rescue it.

events.title: Nicaraguan indigenous leader dies after three years in prison
events.description: Rights groups condemn the death of Brooklyn Rivera, 73, following his long arbitrary detention by the governing regime.

events.title: The drivers risking death on Ukraine's most dangerous bus routes
events.description: Russian drones are targeting public buses in Kherson, killing three transport workers so far this year.

events.title: Grammy-winning director explores his Nigerian grandfather's role in the Biafran war
events.description: Filmmaker Meji Alabi directs a landmark BBC Africa Eye documentary about Nigeria's civil war.

events.title: Ethiopia to vote in first elections since Tigray peace deal
events.description: Four years after the formal end of Ethiopia's Tigray war, Ethiopians will be voting in general elections.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ethiopia to vote in first elections since Tigray peace deal

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Bound by blood: new film highlights Jamaica’s outlawed obeah belief system
events.description: Stew Peas focuses on obeah, an enduring African magic practice in Jamaica banned by colonisers in the 1700s A new movie from award-winning Jamaican film-maker Sosiessia Nixon shines a spotlight on Jamaica’s enduring west African-based magic and spiritual healing tradition known as obeah. Nixon’s tense, feature-length suspense, Stew Peas , tells of the story of Jamaican detective Tessa, who is obsessed with an old murder case. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bound by blood: new film highlights Jamaica’s outlawed obeah belief system

events.title: Massive Attack to tour Australia for first time in 16 years
events.description: The Bristol trip-hop group will perform in Brisbane, Melbourne and Sydney in August Massive Attack are set to tour Australia for the first time in 16 years. The influential British trip-hop group, made up of Robert “3D” Del Naja and Grant “Daddy G” Marshall, will play Brisbane, Melbourne and Sydney in August. The upcoming tour will be the band’s fourth appearance in Australia and their first Australian shows since 2010. Continue reading...

events.title: How aid cuts are hampering the frontline response to the Ebola crisis
events.description: Aid workers in Uganda are watching the Ebola crisis unfold in the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How aid cuts are hampering the frontline response to the Ebola crisis

events.title: Ethiopia votes as ruling party eyes landslide win
events.description: Ethiopian Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is running unopposed in several constituencies. Opposition parties have warned that the elections are even less open than previous votes.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Ethiopia votes as ruling party eyes landslide win

events.title: Middle East: Kuwait hit by missiles and drones
events.description: Kuwait has come under aerial attack, while the US has been hitting military sites in Iran. The Iranian Revolutionary Guard says it has hit a US base in response. Follow DW for the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East: Kuwait hit by missiles and drones

events.title: Middle East: Israel captures key fortress amid Lebanon push
events.description: Israeli troops have captured the strategically important Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon after heavy clashes with Hezbollah. Meanwhile, Trump has toughened the terms for a deal to end the war with Iran. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East: Israel captures key fortress amid Lebanon push

events.title: Japan and China trade accusations at Shangri-La Dialogue
events.description: Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi has strongly rejected China's claim that Japan is moving toward “neo-militarism.” He accused Beijing of rapidly arming itself.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan and China trade accusations at Shangri-La Dialogue

events.title: Macron eyes billions in AI investment at 'Choose France' summit
events.description: French President Emmanuel Macron hosts his annual "Choose France" investment summit on Monday with foreign investors already pledging billions of dollars of capital for AI and data projects. The conference could also see announcements on rare earth investments in France.

events.title: Colombia's presidential race goes to a runoff: De la Espriella vs. Cepeda
events.description: Colombians voted Sunday in the first round of presidential elections, choosing between a reformist left seeking to retain power and a hard-line right promising security amid escalating violence by armed groups. With 99.99% of votes counted, far-right outsider Abelardo de la Espriella leads with more than 10 million votes (43.70%), followed by leftist Iván Cepeda with 9.6 million (40.93%). Amid mutual accusations, the two will face off in a runoff on June 21.

events.title: What is Lebanon's Beaufort Castle, and why have Israeli forces captured it?
events.description: The Israeli army has taken control of Beaufort castle in Southern Lebanon, crossing over the Litani river, which they had been using as a de facto boundary. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called the operation a "dramatic shift" in the Israeli offensive in Lebanon. The Israeli army now has declared all land between the Litani and Zaharani River a conflict zone. Details by Caroline Baum.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What is Lebanon's Beaufort Castle, and why have Israeli forces captured it?

events.title: 'More than 45' killed after blast at building storing explosives in Myanmar
events.description: Scores of people have been killed following a blast at a building in northeastern Myanmar.

events.title: Ex-Iowa school superintendent sentenced to prison as an illegal alien found with firearms
events.description: Iowa's largest school district ex-superintendent sentenced to prison for illegally possessing firearms while living in the U.S. as an illegal alien.

events.title: Eight riders left dangling atop 100-foot roller coaster for over three hours at Texas amusement park
events.description: Eight riders were left dangling atop the Iron Shark roller coaster at Pleasure Pier in Galveston, Texas, for over three hours before being rescued.

events.title: Hezbollah's 'game changing' night-hunting weapon punches through Israel's defenses: expert

events.title: English cops cuffed teen stabbing victim after attacker claimed racial assault

events.title: PSG fans await Champions League trophy celebration in Paris
events.description: Paris Saint-Germain are the champions of Europe after their win on penalties against Arsenal last night. This afternoon, the team heads back to the French capital for a victory parade in the heart of the city. Supporters are already gathering around the Eiffel Tower to welcome them. Details by Emily Boyle.

events.title: Fireworks reportedly trigger mass horse stampede through Rome streets, injuring several soldiers

events.title: Nigerian retired general abducted with his wife in the north-west

events.title: ‘Before, the land sustained us’: Who benefits from Guinea’s bauxite wealth?
events.description: The country has vast reserves of the ore, a source material for aluminium. But citizens still languish in poverty.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Alarm at Mexico bill allowing elections to be annulled for ‘foreign interference’
events.description: Opposition says constitutional amendment would give bill ruling party carte blanche to overturn will of voters Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Amid fierce criticism from opposition groups, Mexico ’s senate has passed ‌a constitutional amendment to include “foreign interference” as grounds to annul election results in the country. The bill, which was presented by the country’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, defines foreign interference as “illicit financing, propaganda, the systematic ⁠dissemination of misinformation, digital manipulation, and ⁠the intervention of foreign governments ⁠or agencies”. Continue reading...

events.title: Chinese dissident says he was berated by ‘pro-regime’ interpreter for UK police
events.description: Hong Qi, who orchestrated protest against Communist government, claims interpreter on 101 call launched political tirade A Chinese dissident who orchestrated an anti-government protest in China after fleeing to the UK has claimed that a “pro-regime” interpreter used by a British police force berated him when he sought help. Hong Qi, who made headlines last year after using a mobile phone while in the UK to remotely project anti-regime slogans on to a building in his home city, Chongqing, contacted police after discovering that his bank accounts had been frozen. Continue reading...

events.title: Jim Chalmers says fewer homes selling at auction may be a ‘good thing’ for first-time buyers
events.description: Auction success hit a new low for the year on the last Saturday in May, with just 54.5% of homes sold Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The treasurer, Jim Chalmers, has suggested falling auction clearance rates may be a “good thing” for first home buyers if it means they aren’t competing against as many property investors. After data was released showing home prices in Australia’s capital cities had begun to fall and buyers were abandoning auctions, Chalmers on Monday said Labor’s proposed property tax reforms weren’t the only thing slowing the housing market. Continue reading...

Thousands of people celebrated across France throughout the night but the parties were blighted by incidents, clashes with police, vandalism, a deadly road accident and hundreds of arrests, according to authorities.

events.title: The week in pictures: Europe's shock early heatwave, French Open battles and the Blue Origin blast
events.description: Former title holders crashed out at Paris's Roland-Garros tennis stadium and PSG faced off with Arsenal in Budapest to win their second Champions League final in a row as a heatwave shattered European temperature records for the month of May: FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Germany news: Most expect AfD state premier after elections
events.description: Most people in Germany expect a premier from the far-right AfD to lead at least one state after elections in the autumn, a survey has found. The Brenner Pass linking Austria and Italy has reopened.

events.title: If Cuba collapses, Trump will be forced to deal with the consequences of his actions
events.description: It's only a couple of months or so since I was last in Cuba, and conditions then were pretty dire: power cuts, large queues for bread and fuel, state-run grocery stores virtually empty, mounting piles of rubbish on street corners, and few foreign visitors.

events.title: Kanye West banned from performing in Italian city

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: The German town where locals spend play money to cut CO2
events.description: It started as a school project to promote local businesses in a remote Bavarian region. Now, the "Chiemgauer" currency helps to cut emissions.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
events.description: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeks The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella won the first round of Colombia’s presidential election on Sunday and will face senator Iván Cepeda, the candidate backed by leftwing president Gustavo Petro, in the runoff. With 99.97% of ballots counted, the outsider and Donald Trump admirer Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014. events.description: Paris Saint-Germain has won back-to-back Champions League titles by beating Arsenal on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest.

events.title: France intercepts Russian-linked oil tanker in Atlantic
events.description: France has intercepted a sanctioned tanker in the Atlantic Ocean as it steps up efforts to target Russia's "shadow fleet." Naval officials said the ship was sailing under a false flag.

events.title: India news: Virat Kohli fulfils long-held dream with winning six for RCB in IPL final
events.description: Royal Challengers Bengaluru brought their title chase to a dramatic close with a six from superstar Kohli sealing the match against the Gujarat Titans. DW has the latest.

events.title: DNA had one rule. Bacteria didn’t get the memo
events.description: Every cell in every organism on Earth copies DNA the same way. Except one bacterial protein — quietly doing something scientists had never seen before.

events.title: Nicaragua: Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera dies in detention
events.description: The Indigenous leader and former lawmaker had been detained by Nicaragua's authoritarian government in 2023 on undisclosed charges.

events.title: Colombia set for runoff vote after tight presidential poll
events.description: Right-wing outsider Abelardo de la Espreilla seems set to face veteran leftist candidate Ivan Cepeda in the second round of the Colombia presidential election, but Cepeda seemed to question the preliminary results.

events.title: Germany’s thriving Alevi community
events.description: The Alevis make up the fourth largest religious community in Germany today. What are the main tenets of their faith? And how are they preserving their unique culture?

events.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
events.description: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

events.title: Malta's Labour Party wins historic fourth term: preliminary results
events.description: Prime Minister Robert Abela, who campaigned on the strength of the economy, had been widely expected to win the vote.

events.title: Swing Youth: In Nazi Germany, jazz was an act of defiance
events.description: The Nazis denounced jazz music as "degenerate art," despite its widespread popularity in Germany. As the Nazis clamped down on expression, groups of jazz-loving teenagers formed the Swing Youth to rebel.

events.title: 'Animal medicine': Therapy donkeys help patients at French psychiatric hospital
events.description: Patients staying in a psychiatric hospital near Paris have been singing the praises of a novel treatment: spending time with therapy donkeys. Experts and patients say caring for the animals, which are known for their calm and social nature, helps improve emotional regulation, communication, social interaction and self-esteem.

events.title: PSG parade champions league trophy as they celebrate back-to-back titles
events.description: Paris Saint-Germain brought the Champions League trophy back to France, taking it from the Champ-de-Mars to the Parc des Princes.

events.title: Colombia's election: Meeting the Presidential contenders
events.description: The Economist’s Kinley Salmon on his encounters with the candidates

events.title: Vingegaard completes Grand Tour set with victory at the Giro d'Italia
events.description: Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard won the 2026 Giro d'Italia on Sunday to become only the eighth male rider in history to win all three Grand Tours, having also won the Tour de France twice before his triumph at the Vuelta a Espana last year.

events.title: Huge crowds greet PSG players as they parade Champions League trophy in Paris
events.description: No description available.

events.title: A 'second brain' on your face: testing the AI glasses built by Meta's hackers
events.description: They first made headlines by bolting facial recognition onto Meta's Ray-Bans to show how easily the technology could identify strangers in the street. Now Anhphu Nguyen and Caine Ardayfio have a product of their own: Mira, a $650 pair of AI glasses, with no camera this time, that quietly transcribes and summarises everything, so you can turn your life into a searchable database.

events.title: US, UK and Australia to develop underwater drones through defence pact
events.description: Unmanned undersea vehicles will be developed under the AUKUS defence pact, US secretary of defence Pete Hegseth said.

events.title: Some young Spaniards embrace Catholicism and can’t wait for Pope Leo’s visit
events.description: Traveling to Spain in June and France in September, Pope Leo XIV will find traditionally Catholic but now staunchly secular countries with an abundance of historic churches but few people in the pews

events.title: Patients find help with therapy donkeys at psychiatric hospital near Paris
events.description: Therapy donkeys are helping patients with mental health conditions at a psychiatric hospital near Paris

events.title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party
events.description: Polls opened Monday in Ethiopia in an election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party

events.title: Voting begins in Ethiopia where Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to win a majority of seats
events.description: Voting begins in Ethiopia where Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to win a majority of seats

events.title: Colombia's presidential election, in photos
events.description: Colombians voted to select a successor to President Gustavo Petro in an election Sunday, as armed guards and police kept watch. Candidates Iván Cepeda and Abelardo de la Espriella advanced to the second round. ___ This gallery was curated by AP photo editors. ___ AP photography: https://apnews.com/photography AP News on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/apnews

events.title: Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera dies in Nicaragua after nearly 3 years of detention
events.description: Renowned Indigenous leader Brooklyn River, who spent years fighting for the rights of his community and was imprisoned by the government in September 2023, has died
events.ticker_label: 💥 Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera dies in Nicaragua after nearly 3 years of detention

events.title: Venice's growing flamingo population finds refuge in recovering wetlands
events.description: Flamingos are flocking to the Venetian Lagoon in record numbers as ecological efforts restore damaged wetlands

events.title: Ethiopia heads to the polls for an election expected to be dominated again by Abiy's ruling party
events.description: Ethiopians are preparing to vote in an election that the ruling party is expected to win by a landslide
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Ethiopia heads to the polls for an election expected to be dominated again by Abiy's ruling party

events.title: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
events.description: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps

events.title: ICE arrests illegal alien who allegedly faked asylum claim based on homosexuality, became Indiana jail officer
events.description: A suspected illegal immigrant allegedly used a fraudulent asylum claim to enter the U.S. and was later hired as a corrections officer in Indiana, DHS says.

events.title: Scott Peterson's longtime lawyer claims 'new' evidence could force courts to revisit his murder conviction
events.description: Scott Peterson's lawyer claims new evidence could force another look at his murder conviction, ahead of a summer documentary about the high-profile case.

events.title: NYPD commissioner 'proudly' leads Israel parade as grand marshal; Mamdani breaks tradition, avoids event
events.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani chose to skip NYC's annual Israel Day parade as Gov. Kathy Hochul, Chuck Schumer and other political leaders attended the event.

events.title: FBI hunts most wanted neighbor next door who allegedly faked cancer, scammed loved ones for luxury getaways
events.description: A Pennsylvania woman is wanted by the FBI after allegedly faking brain cancer to scam friends and family out of thousands used for Australian vacations.

events.title: Fox News Campus Radicals Newsletter: Jewish student abuse alleged, disrespecting Charlie Kirk, woke work
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News Campus Radicals newsletter.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
events.description: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst

events.title: Anti-cartel hardliner channels Trump in bid to end Colombia's leftist era in pivotal election
events.description: Colombia's next president could reshape U.S. counternarcotics cooperation as voters choose between security-first and dialogue-based approaches.

events.title: Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges
events.description: France recorded 1,320 antisemitic acts in 2025, up from 436 in 2022, as Jewish families alter daily habits to avoid being identified, CRIF's president says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges
